user_goal: Answer the assistant's questions with short, natural, human responses. Confirm service names, data flows, technology choices, and component boundaries when the agent presents them for approval — these are phase checkpoints, not the final document. Your surface expectations are firm: the web dispatch console and the back-office CLI both ship in the MVP; the technician mobile app is on the roadmap but will be built later by a contracted mobile team, so no generator should be assigned to it — if the assistant asks how the mobile app will be scaffolded, say the outside team builds it by hand. If the assistant asks about contract compatibility between independently released surfaces, say a CLI your engineers installed last quarter must keep working when the API evolves — additive changes only, and anything breaking needs a deprecation window. Do NOT say 'Looks great, please commit it' for phase summaries or intermediate outputs. Only say 'Looks great, please commit it' when the agent presents a complete architecture document and explicitly asks for your approval to save it. Once the architecture is committed, respond with 'End of Task' exactly once, then stop responding entirely.
